title: ～ながら
titleEn: Express doing two things simultaneously or at the same time.
explanation: Express doing two things simultaneously or at the same time. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ながら grammar point is used to express the concept of doing two things simultaneously or at the same time. In English, it can be translated as 'while', 'as', or 'during'. ～ながら can be attached to the stem of a verb to convey this meaning.

title: ～にあたり
titleEn: Expresses the meaning of 'at the time of', 'in', 'on', or 'upon'.
explanation: Expresses the meaning of 'at the time of', 'in', 'on', or 'upon'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～にあたり grammar point is used to describe an important event or situation that occurs at a specific moment. It can be translated as 'at the time of', 'in', 'on', or 'upon' in English. The formation is quite simple, as it only requires a noun before it.

title: ～において
titleEn: Indicates the place, time, or context where an action or event occurs; 'in', 'at', 'on', 'regarding'.
explanation: Indicates the place, time, or context where an action or event occurs; 'in', 'at', 'on', 'regarding'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～において grammar point is used to show the place, time, or context where an action or event takes place. It can be translated as 'in', 'at', 'on', or 'regarding' in English. It is often used in formal situations and can be replaced by more casual equivalents such as で, に, or は in informal contexts.

title: ～にかかわらず
titleEn: Regardless of; irrespective of; (often used as) 'whether or not'.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + にかかわらず, い-Adjective + にかかわらず, な-Adjective + にかかわらず, Noun + にかかわらず
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + にかかわらず, い-Adjective + にかかわらず, な-Adjective + にかかわらず, Noun + にかかわらず
explanation: Regardless of; irrespective of; (often used as) 'whether or not'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～にかかわらず grammar point is used to express that something happens or is true regardless of a particular fact, condition, or circumstance. It can be translated as 'regardless of', 'irrespective of', or 'whether or not'. It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, or nouns.

title: ～にかけては
titleEn: Indicates a specific area or aspect in which someone or something excels.
explanation: Indicates a specific area or aspect in which someone or something excels. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ～にかけては is used to emphasize a particular area or aspect in which someone or something excels or is exceptional. This structure is often used with positive connotations regarding the subject's expertise or outstanding qualities.

title: ～にしたがって
titleEn: Expresses a natural consequence or a change that occurs according to something else; 'as', 'with', 'in accordance with'.
explanation: Expresses a natural consequence or a change that occurs according to something else; 'as', 'with', 'in accordance with'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～にしたがって grammar point is used to express a natural consequence or a change that occurs according to something else. It can be translated as 'as', 'with', or 'in accordance with' in English. This grammar point is used with nouns and can be used to show a change in proportion or in accordance with a rule or principle.

title: ～にしたら
titleEn: Express a point of view; 'from the perspective of', 'if I were'.
structure: Noun + にしたら, Pronoun + にしたら
structureEn: Noun + にしたら, Pronoun + にしたら
explanation: Express a point of view; 'from the perspective of', 'if I were'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～にしたら grammar point is used to express a point of view or opinion from someone else's perspective. It can be translated as 'from the perspective of' or 'if I were' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a noun or a pronoun.

title: ～にしろ～にしろ
titleEn: Expresses 'either way', 'in any case', 'be it...', used when presenting two alternatives.
structure: Verb (dictionary form or negative form) + にしろ, い-Adjective + にしろ, な-Adjective + にしろ, Noun + にしろ
structureEn: Verb (dictionary form or negative form) + にしろ, い-Adjective + にしろ, な-Adjective + にしろ, Noun + にしろ
explanation: Expresses 'either way', 'in any case', 'be it...', used when presenting two alternatives. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～にしろ～にしろ grammar point is used to present two alternatives and emphasize that regardless of which one is chosen or occurs, the result or conclusion remains the same. It can be translated as 'either way', 'in any case', or 'be it...'. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.
